article_meta.title: — News —'Locked And Loaded': Trump Warns U.S

transcript.content:
You are a news summarization assistant. Summarize the following article objectively and extract its key facts. Respond ONLY with a valid JSON object — no prose, no markdown fences:
{
  "summary": "string (150-200 words, neutral tone)",
  "key_facts": ["string","string","string","string","string"],
  "sources_represented": ["string"]
}
Rules:
- summary must be 150-200 words
- key_facts must contain EXACTLY 5 items — the 5 most objectively significant facts
- sources_represented must list every distinct perspective or source type included
- Do not editorialize, adopt the article's framing, or use loaded language
- Represent all perspectives present in the article proportionally

Summarize this article:

---
HEADLINE: — News —'Locked And Loaded': Trump Warns U.S
SOURCE: Morning Wire

ARTICLE:

— News —'Locked And Loaded': Trump Warns U.S. Will 'Rescue' Iranians If Regime Opens Fire On ProtestersThe Islamic Republic has been rocked by demonstrations over its collapsing economy. By Leif Le Mahieu•Jan 2, 2026 DailyWire.com•FacebookXMailCredit: Photo by Jim WATSON / AFP via Getty Images. President Donald Trump said that the United States would come to the "rescue" if Iranian forces began shooting protesters, as demonstrations against the regime continued for a sixth consecutive day. Sparked by economic unrest, protests first broke out last weekend in Tehran with groups chanting "death to the dictator" and "freedom." Seven people, including protesters and government forces, have been killed so far, according to reports. "If Iran shots [sic] and violently kills peaceful protesters, which is their custom, the United States of America will come to their rescue," Trump posted on Truth Social early Friday morning. "We are locked and loaded and ready to go. Thank you for your attention to this matter!"Trump's warning comes as he has continued to take an aggressive approach to Iran, saying earlier this week that he would support more Israeli military strikes on the country if Tehran continues to develop ballistic missiles or pursue nuclear capabilities.In response to Trump's post, the secretary of Iran's Supreme National Security Council said any intervention by America would exacerbate instability in the area while accusing the United States and Israel of encouraging the disorder. "With the statements by Israeli officials and Donald Trump, what has been going on behind the scenes is now clear. We distinguish between the stance of the protesting shopkeepers and the actions of disruptive actors, and Trump should know that U.S. interference in this internal matter would mean destabilizing the entire region and destroying America's interests," he posted on X. "The American people should know — Trump started this adventurism. They should be mindful of their soldiers' safety."Christmas Sale – Get 40% off New DailyWire+ Annual MembershipsMuch of the violence has occurred in Western Iran, where a local official promised that protesters would be treated "decisively and without leniency," Reuters reported. Dozens of protesters, including women and children, have been arrested. On Thursday, Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian said the protests were the result of the government's failure to address skyrocketing inflation and widespread economic anxiety. "We are to blame… Do not look for America or anyone else to blame. We must serve properly so that people are satisfied with us…. It is us who have to find a solution to these problems," he said, Reuters reported. Iran's currency has reportedly dropped to 1.42 million to the dollar, a loss of more than 56% over six months, fueling soaring inflation and driving food prices up 72% from last year.Create a free account to join the conversation!Start CommentingAlready have an account?Log inGot a tip worth investigating?Your information could be the missing piece to an important story. Submit your tip today and make a difference.Submit TipThe Daily Wire > Read > 'Locked And Loaded': Trump Warns U.S. Will 'Rescue' Iranians If Regime Opens Fire On Protesters
---

```json
{
  "summary": "President Donald Trump warned on Truth Social that the United States would intervene if Iranian security forces opened fire on anti-government protesters, stating America is "locked and loaded and ready to go." The demonstrations, now in their sixth consecutive day, began last weekend in Tehran driven by severe economic conditions including 72% food price increases and a currency that has lost over 56% of its value in six months. Seven people have been killed so far, and dozens have been arrested. Iran's Supreme National Security Council secretary accused the U.S. and Israel of inciting unrest and warned that American intervention would destabilize the region. Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian acknowledged government responsibility for the economic crisis, stating officials must address skyrocketing inflation and widespread anxiety rather than blaming external actors.",
  "key_facts": [
    "President Trump threatened U.S. military intervention if Iran violently suppresses ongoing anti-government protests that have entered their sixth day",
    "Protests erupted over severe economic conditions, with Iran's currency losing over 56% of its value in six months and food prices rising 72% year-over-year",
    "Seven people including protesters and government forces have been killed, with dozens arrested including women and children",
    "Iran's Supreme National Security Council accused the U.S. and Israel of encouraging disorder and warned intervention would destabilize the entire region",
    "Iranian President Pezeshkian publicly accepted government responsibility for the economic crisis rather than blaming foreign powers"
  ],
  "sources_represented": [
    "U.S. Presidential administration",
    "Iranian Supreme National Security Council",
    "Iranian President",
    "Reuters news reporting",
    "Daily Wire editorial perspective"
  ]
}
```

parsed_output.summary: President Donald Trump warned on Truth Social that the United States would intervene if Iranian security forces opened fire on anti-government protesters, stating America is "locked and loaded and ready to go." The demonstrations, now in their sixth consecutive day, began last weekend in Tehran driven by severe economic conditions including 72% food price increases and a currency that has lost over 56% of its value in six months. Seven people have been killed so far, and dozens have been arrested. Iran's Supreme National Security Council secretary accused the U.S. and Israel of inciting unrest and warned that American intervention would destabilize the region. Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian acknowledged government responsibility for the economic crisis, stating officials must address skyrocketing inflation and widespread anxiety rather than blaming external actors.
key_facts: President Trump threatened U.S. military intervention if Iran violently suppresses ongoing anti-government protests that have entered their sixth day
Protests erupted over severe economic conditions, with Iran's currency losing over 56% of its value in six months and food prices rising 72% year-over-year
Seven people including protesters and government forces have been killed, with dozens arrested including women and children
Iran's Supreme National Security Council accused the U.S. and Israel of encouraging disorder and warned intervention would destabilize the entire region
Iranian President Pezeshkian publicly accepted government responsibility for the economic crisis rather than blaming foreign powers
